headlines.title: US Carries out 10th Night of Strikes After Service Members Killed
headlines.description: United States Central Command released video of its 10th night of consecutive strikes flying deep into Iran, targeting command centers, launch sites and air defenses. President Donald Trump says Iran “will pay” anytime they kill an American servicemember. Meanwhile, the State Department issued a rare global travel warning to Americans abroad after Iran sent another barrage of missiles into Jordan. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: How AI is Transforming the Way China’s Microdramas Are Made
headlines.description: Microdramas made specifically for phones have become a huge sensation in China and are finding fans in the United States. As China’s short series go global, artificial intelligence is transforming not just how their stories are told but how entertainment itself is made. NBC’s Janis Mackey Frayer reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump Targets Canada With 50% Tariff Citing Trade Discrimination
headlines.description: President Donald Trump has announced a new 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, accusing the country of discriminating against American-made products. Meantime, the White House says the new Air Force One, gifted by Qatar, will be sent away for upgrades, raising new questions about how safe it is for the president. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Andy Burnham formally appointed U.K. prime minster
headlines.description: Andy Burnham was formally appointed the newest British prime minster, the seventh to take up the position in the last decade. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez reports on how Burnham differs from outgoing prime minster Keir Starmer and his meeting with King Charles III.

headlines.title: Spain’s supremacy, Messi and Mbappé’s race for greatness, and a freak injury: World Cup quiz
headlines.description: President Donald Trump makes a call to FIFA, Scotland fans make an impression, and an upset results in a national holiday.

headlines.title: FDA says it’s still investigating Taylor Farms in cyclosporiasis outbreak
headlines.description: Taylor Farms continues to be the subject of a Food and Drug Administration investigation into the growing cyclosporiasis outbreak, despite a false positive test result, the agency said Monday.

headlines.title: We want to hear how Medicaid work requirements are affecting you
headlines.description: If you have lost your health coverage or are at risk of losing it, tell us your story.

headlines.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
headlines.description: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

headlines.title: Spain’s supremacy, Messi and Mbappé’s race for greatness, and a freak injury: World Cup quiz
headlines.description: President Donald Trump makes a call to FIFA, Scotland fans make an impression, and an upset results in a national holiday.

headlines.title: Father, son charged with kidnapping 2 U.S. Forest Service biologists at gunpoint
headlines.description: Joseph and Phoenix Henrichsen were charged with kidnapping a federal employee. “I’ve taken two fed biologists hostage,” the father is accused of saying in a voicemail.

headlines.title: Here’s how often you should change your towels, and when to throw them away, according to experts
headlines.description: I talked with laundry experts to learn how often you should change ans wash your towels and when it’s best to throw them away in exchange for new ones.

headlines.title: Everyone is talking about exosomes in skin care — here’s why
headlines.description: Think of exosomes as microscopic delivery trucks that help repair skin tissue, increase collagen and more. I spoke to dermatologists about what exosomes do and how to safely use them.
